_comment: Data source for bin/install_engine.sh — NOT a paste target. The installer substitutes {{PYTHON_PATH}} and {{CLAUDE_KB_HOME}} and merges these blocks into ~/.claude/settings.json. NOTE: the MCP server is intentionally NOT here. Claude Code does NOT read MCP servers from settings.json (only from the `claude mcp` registry / a project .mcp.json); install_engine registers it with `claude mcp add --scope user` instead. settings.json IS read for hooks and permissions, which is why those live here. Fresh installs use the latch MCP name; claude-kb remains a supported legacy alias.

permissions._comment: Server-level rules auto-approve every tool the latch MCP server exposes (current and future) — no per-tool prompts, nothing to keep in sync. The legacy claude-kb rule remains during the compatibility rename so existing registrations are not under-approved.

UserPromptSubmit._comment: Hook embeds via the MCP server's loopback listener, not in-process. If the daemon hasn't pre-warmed yet (first prompt of a brand-new session), embed_remote returns None and the hook skips retrieval cleanly rather than blocking on cold-load.

PostToolUse._comment: Deterministic activity surface: parses kb_activity/findings from the KB tool result and emits the one-line summary on systemMessage (the user-visible channel; KB id=552), independent of whether the agent echoes it. Scoped to the latch MCP tools.
Legacy activity surface for existing claude-kb MCP registrations during the compatibility rename.
